bullets.text: Federal Reserve seeks comment on codifying removal of “reputation risk” from bank supervision, potentially narrowing examiner discretion and compliance burdens.
Supreme Court tariff ruling triggers refund and legal uncertainty; FedEx sues for tariff refunds while Congress debates reclaiming tariff-setting authority.
EU postpones vote on U.S. trade deal after renewed global tariff threats, raising near-term transatlantic trade friction risk.
Panama voids China-linked port contracts and hands canal terminals to Maersk and MSC, underscoring strategic competition over critical maritime chokepoints.
Post-cartel-leader killing violence in Mexico disrupts trucking, ports, air freight and tourism routes, creating cross-border supply-chain and security spillovers.
China keeps benchmark lending rates unchanged, signaling tolerance for a stronger yuan despite slowing growth—tightening financial conditions versus easing expectations.
U.S. mortgage rates drop below 6% for first time since 2022, improving affordability and potentially reviving refinancing and housing demand.
Jamie Dimon warns lofty asset prices elevate economic risk, reinforcing concerns about fragile valuations if growth slows or rates stay higher for longer.
Anthropic-driven “AI disruption” narrative hits software and cybersecurity stocks; IBM falls on fears new tooling accelerates legacy-code replacement and margin pressure.
Amazon plans $12B Louisiana AI data-center buildout, highlighting escalating hyperscaler capex and power/infrastructure constraints shaping U.S. regional development.
Binance staff found $1.7B in crypto sent to Iranian entities, spotlighting sanctions-evasion risk and rising compliance costs for exchanges.
ASML unveils improved EUV light source that could raise chip output ~50% by 2030, strengthening semiconductor supply outlook amid export-control geopolitics.
Scientists report a universal nasal-spray vaccine candidate protecting against COVID, flu, and pneumonia, potentially shifting respiratory prevention toward broad mucosal immunity.
New blood test forecasts Alzheimer’s years before symptoms, enabling earlier intervention trials and potentially reshaping screening economics and long-term care planning.
